questions.question: Define data structures and explain their importance in computer science.
keyPoints: Data structure is a way of organizing and storing data
Enables efficient access and manipulation of data
Forms foundation for algorithm design
Crucial for software development and optimization

questions.question: Differentiate between primitive and non-primitive data structures with examples.
keyPoints: Primitive are basic types (int, float, char) provided by language
Non-primitive are user-defined (arrays, linked lists, trees)
Primitive store single values, non-primitive store collections
Examples: int, char vs array, linked list, stack

questions.question: What is an Abstract Data Type (ADT)? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: ADT defines data type by its behavior not implementation
Specifies operations without how they are performed
Example: Stack ADT with push, pop, peek operations
Hides internal representation from user

questions.question: Explain the concept of time complexity and space complexity.
keyPoints: Time complexity measures execution time as function of input size
Space complexity measures memory required
Both expressed using Big O notation
Help evaluate efficiency of algorithms and data structures

questions.question: Distinguish between linear and non-linear data structures.
keyPoints: Linear: elements arranged sequentially, each has unique predecessor/successor
Non-linear: elements have hierarchical/network relationships
Linear examples: arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues
Non-linear examples: trees, graphs

questions.question: What are the basic operations performed on data structures?
keyPoints: Insertion: adding new elements
Deletion: removing elements
Traversal: accessing all elements
Searching: finding specific elements
Sorting: arranging elements in order

questions.question: Explain static and dynamic data structures with suitable examples.
keyPoints: Static: fixed size at compile time, memory allocated contiguously
Dynamic: size can change during runtime, memory allocated dynamically
Arrays are static, linked lists are dynamic
Static offers faster access, dynamic offers flexibility

questions.question: Explain Big O notation and discuss various time complexities with examples.
keyPoints: Big O describes upper bound of algorithm complexity
O(1): constant time - array access by index
O(log n): logarithmic - binary search
O(n): linear - linear search, traversal
O(n²): quadratic - nested loops, bubble sort
O(2^n): exponential - recursive Fibonacci
Importance in choosing appropriate data structures

questions.question: Discuss the criteria for selecting an appropriate data structure for an application.
keyPoints: Nature of data - type, size, operations needed
Time complexity requirements for operations
Space complexity considerations
Ease of implementation and maintenance
Memory availability and access patterns
Example: frequent insertions at beginning - linked list over array

questions.question: Explain the relationship between data structures and algorithms. How does choice of data structure affect algorithm performance?
keyPoints: Data structures store data, algorithms manipulate it
Choice of data structure significantly impacts efficiency
Right structure can reduce time complexity drastically
Example: using hash table reduces search from O(n) to O(1)
Data structure should match algorithm requirements
Design decisions must consider both together
